name: Chocolate Pound Cake
recipeInstructions:
Heat oven to 350 degrees F. Grease and flour a 10-inch tube pan. In a large bowl, combine the sugar, 1 cup of margarine, shortening, and 1 teaspoon of vanilla; beat until light and fluffy. Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition. In a medium bowl, combine flour, cocoa powder, baking powder, and salt. Alternately add the flour mixture and milk to the sugar mixture, beginning and ending with the flour mixture, and beat well after each addition. Pour the batter into the greased and floured pan.
Bake at 350 degrees F for 70 to 85 minutes or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean. Cool upright in the pan for 25 minutes; invert onto a serving plate. Allow the cake to cool completely.
In a small saucepan, combine 2 tablespoons of cocoa, water, and butter or margarine. Cook over low heat until the mixture thickens, stirring constantly. Remove from heat. Stir in vanilla and powdered sugar; beat until smooth. Spread glaze over the top of the cooled cake, allowing some to run down the sides.
Yield: 16 servings.
HIGH ALTITUDE Above 3500 Feet: Increase flour to 3-1/4 cups. Bake at 375 degrees F for 70 to 80 minutes.
